identitySentence: Alagood Cartwright Burke PC has served Denton since 2003, bringing together three board-certified partners for real estate, civil litigation, personal injury, business law, and probate matters.

concept.headline: Trusted Legal Advice for Many Areas of Life, Right Here in Denton
concept.subheadline: Whether you are dealing with a real estate matter, a business dispute, an injury, or a will, you can reach a board-certified Denton attorney without phone tag. Share what happened and choose a consultation time that fits your schedule.
credibilityPoints: Serving Denton County since 2003 from offices at 319 W. Oak Street
All three named partners board-certified by the Texas Board of Legal Specialization (real estate, personal injury trial law, and civil trial law)
Founding partners practicing law since 1987 and 1992
Home to a past President of the Denton County Bar Association and a Best of Denton 2012 award recipient

sections.title: Practice focus
sections.body: This is a full-service civil practice: commercial and residential real estate, civil litigation, corporate and business law, construction, employment matters, and condemnation and eminent domain. The firm also represents injury clients in motor vehicle, trucking, medical malpractice, and nursing home negligence cases, and it handles wills, probate, mediation, and arbitration.

sections.title: Denton roots
sections.body: Founded in Denton in 2003, the firm practices from 319 W. Oak Street and serves clients across Denton County and nearby communities including Celina, Lewisville, Pilot Point, and Prosper. One of its founding partners is a past President of the Denton County Bar Association.

sections.title: What to expect
sections.body: At your consultation, an attorney will ask what happened, review any documents you bring, and explain your options in plain language. You will get an honest assessment of your matter, including whether this firm is the right fit for it.

outreachDraft.subject: Proof matters more than promises: 15 days, 1 demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.
outreachDraft.body:
DRAFT — NOT SENT. Review and authorize before any outreach.

To the attorneys and team at Alagood Cartwright Burke PC:

Your firm has been part of Denton's legal community since 2003, and all three of your named partners hold board certifications from the Texas Board of Legal Specialization. That depth of credentialing is uncommon for a firm your size, and it deserves an intake process that matches it.

Your website already does a lot right: clear practice-area pages, repeated invitations to schedule a consultation, and a contact form right on the homepage. What it cannot do today is turn a late-night inquiry into a confirmed, deposit-backed appointment before your office opens. Scheduling still depends on the contact form or a phone call during business hours. For a firm handling real estate transactions, injury claims, business disputes, and probate matters, the people who reach out after hours are often the ones whose problems feel most urgent.

That is the gap your Lead Capturer closes. We built a working proof specifically for Alagood Cartwright Burke PC, including a custom contact form and a custom identity line written for your practice:

"Alagood Cartwright Burke PC has served Denton since 2003, bringing together three board-certified partners for real estate, civil litigation, personal injury, business law, and probate matters."

Here is how it works:

- A potential client submits an inquiry through your custom form.
- The inquiry is emailed directly to your firm's inbox. Nothing sits behind a third-party dashboard your staff has to remember to check.
- The potential client automatically receives a response specific to the topic of their message, whether that is a truck accident, a commercial lease, a will, or an eminent domain claim.
- That response presents your available consultation times.
- A $50 reservation deposit confirms the appointment, so your attorneys spend consultation time with people who are committed to showing up.

We are offering a 15-day proof period. Test the complete experience end to end, exactly as a potential client would, before you commit to anything.

How to test your Lead Capturer (15 minutes):

1. Submit a clearly labeled TEST inquiry using a customer email address you control.
2. Do not enter confidential client information.
3. Confirm your firm inbox receives a real email.
4. Confirm the notification contains every displayed form field.
5. Confirm the subject line identifies your firm and the likely matter type.
6. Confirm the Reply-To address contains the submitted customer email.
7. Confirm the system automatically generates a response specific to the TEST inquiry.
8. Confirm that response is sent to the customer email without any staff action.
9. Confirm the customer email account actually receives it.
10. Confirm the response addresses the actual inquiry topic.
11. Confirm genuine available consultation times are presented.
12. Select a time.
13. Confirm the $50 reservation requirement appears.
14. Confirm the preparation list fits the matter.
15. Confirm the prohibited-item list includes legal documents.
16. In a payment sandbox, confirm the appointment locks only after a verified $50 payment event.

If any step fails, the test fails — contact us and we will make it right.

We would rather prove the value in 15 days than promise it in a pitch.

The IntakeBrief Team
[Physical mailing address: provided before any send]

If you would prefer not to hear from us again, reply with 'unsubscribe' and we will remove you immediately.
